bugs.summary: The proposed patch ignores `injectRuntimeTask` opt-in when computing `injectTask`, so non-foreach opt-in stages lose runtime task prompt injection and foreach child metadata no longer carries the opt-in flag.
bugs.impact: Opted-in reduce/support/report-style stages can miss cross-cutting user constraints. For foreach stages, the direct prompt condition still has a foreach-specific opt-in branch, but the emitted `foreach.injectRuntimeTask` metadata is changed from the opt-in value to false.
acceptableFixes: Preserve opt-in runtime task injection for non-task stages while keeping default injection limited.
Add a regression test that opted-in non-task stages and foreach metadata receive runtime task constraints.
mustNotSay: All stages should always receive the runtime task unconditionally.
This only affects prompt text and cannot change review behavior.
The foreach prompt condition itself loses the opt-in branch; it still references `optInInjectRuntimeTask` directly.

mustAvoidClaims: Using prior A/B run notes as evidence.
Claiming the patch must match HEAD instead of evaluating runtime task propagation semantics.

difficulty.rationale: Provider reviews judged this medium: the changed line is local, but correct impact requires tracing non-foreach prompt injection and foreach metadata.

approval.notes: Calibration saturated: all arms scored 1.0. Rewrite with subtler same-root-cause variant before primary benchmark use.
